title: The Worshipful Guild of Stringed-Instrument Luthiers
one_liner: An ornate craft guild website for stringed-instrument luthiers rendered as a leather-bound folio brought to the screen.
concept_summary: An 18th-century editorial-heritage treatise translated to web: engraved frontispiece, two-column justified body with drop caps, hand-set chapter numerals (CAP. II, CAP. III…), foliate rules, wax-seal navigation, and tipped-in plates throughout.
motifs: engraved house marks for member workshops

pages.title: Frontispiece
pages.summary: Title cartouche with the guild's full Latin-and-English name, a MDCCXIV roundel, a centered engraved viola d'amore within an acanthus frame, two-column justified preface from the Warden with drop caps, three plate-card teasers (Apprenticeship, Exhibition, Journal), an instrument-of-the-month strip, and a footer with the guild oath.

pages.title: Of the Member Workshops
pages.summary: Filter ribbon (instrument family, region, accepting apprentices) above a grid of twelve workshop cards each bearing an engraved house mark, master luthier, city, specialties, and founded year. Followed by an engraved foldout map of member locations and a call-to-action to apply for membership.

pages.title: Cap. III — Of the Path to Mastery
pages.summary: Chapter-style page with drop-cap opening, a three-tier timeline (Apprentice → Journeyman → Master) with foliate dividers and marginal footnotes on stipend, bench hours, and examination. Closes with an engraved petition-style application form, complete with wax seal.

pages.title: Cap. IV — Of Woods, Varnishes & Strings
pages.summary: Diderot-plate inspired catalogue: a large labelled cross-section engraving with five numbered callouts, a sortable materials table of ten tonewoods/varnishes/strings (species, grading, sourcing partner, sustainability), a varnish-recipe vignette with engraved bottles, a forester's sidebar note, and an interactive maple-billet rotator switching between quarter-sawn and flat-sawn engravings.

pages.title: Cap. V — The Annual Exhibition
pages.summary: Year and host city cartouche (MMXXVI · Cremona · Palazzo dell'Arte), a Best-in-Show hero plate (the 'Cygnet' Violin) with juror citation, a two-column gallery of six juried instruments with copperplate captions, an attending-workshops list, and an archive carousel of past exhibition frontispieces back to MMXVIII.

pages.title: Instrument of the Month
pages.summary: A tipped-in plate spread: a full engraving of the 'Maréchal' Theorbo (Bologna, 1672) on the verso with a copperplate caption, and on the recto a full entry with maker, year, dimensions, varnish, tonal notes, and a numbered provenance chain styled as a Roman-numeralled lineage. A phonograph-horn play button toggles 'sounding' state. A thumbnail strip of previously featured instruments follows.

pages.title: Journal of Techniques
pages.summary: Engraved periodical layout: masthead with Vol. CCCX, Issue II in Roman numerals; table of contents; lead article 'On the Felting of the Bass-Bar — A Re-examination' in two justified columns with numbered figures (Fig. I, Fig. II), parchment-tooltip footnote pop-overs, Roman-numeralled sections (§ I — § IV), and an author colophon with engraved portrait. A sidebar lists past issues, subscription details, and editorial credits.

interactions: wax-seal hover state on navigation that 'presses' a burgundy seal beneath each label
workshop directory filter chips for instrument family, region, and apprenticeship availability (dims non-matching cards)
interactive maple-billet rotator flipping between quarter-sawn and flat-sawn engravings
footnote pop-overs that surface citations in a parchment tooltip on hover/focus
phonograph-horn play button that toggles a 'sounding' state with subtle animation
mobile hamburger menu opening a parchment drawer
soft fade page-turn transition between chapters
mobile_behavior: Two-column folio collapses to a single justified column; chapter numerals shrink to inline kickers; margin engravings reflow above their related paragraph as half-width plates; the wax-seal nav becomes a hamburger that opens a parchment drawer.
asset_strategy: All engravings are hand-authored inline SVG using hatching patterns, double-rule frames, foliate rosettes, and small-caps inscriptions. No external image downloads. Webfonts loaded from Google Fonts (Cormorant Garamond, IM Fell English, IM Fell English SC) with system serif fallbacks.
required_text_present: The Worshipful Guild of Stringed-Instrument Luthiers
